title: What Codex Is Good At
description: Understand the role Codex plays in an Anydocs authoring workflow.

render.markdown:
# What Codex Is Good At

Codex works best when the task has a clear tool boundary and a concrete output, instead of free-form edits across raw JSON files.

In Anydocs, good Codex tasks include:

- validating the project contract and enabled languages
- finding pages by page id or slug
- creating and updating pages
- changing page status in a controlled way
- checking navigation and page state before and after changes

The main rule is simple: **have Codex call MCP tools instead of guessing the source-file layout and editing JSON directly.**
render.plainText: Codex works best in Anydocs when tasks have a clear tool boundary, such as validation, reading pages, creating pages, updating pages, and changing page status. The key rule is to use MCP tools instead of editing raw JSON directly.
